title: China-to-Chile BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Versus Chile Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Chile project reviewers may accept China GB evidence as supporting technical documentation, but Chile acceptance normally depends on SEC product scope, Chilean installation rules, CNE / Coordinador grid studies, contract specifications, and transport documents. GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 should be positioned as China-origin evidence, not as automatic Chile market-access approvals.
target_standards: CNE / Coordinador Electrico Nacional grid requirements
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 addresses safety requirements for lithium cells and batteries used in electrical energy storage systems. GB/T 36276-2023 addresses lithium-ion batteries for electrical energy storage. Both are highly relevant to BESS exports from China but must be mapped to Chilean product, transport, installation, grid, and project requirements.
rows.gap: Create a crosswalk from GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 clauses to requested IEC, SEC, CNE, Coordinador, transport, and project clauses. Identify missing Spanish documentation, local labels, model files, site fire evidence, and Chile-specific declarations.

mandatory_status.target: Not a Chile mandatory standard by itself unless required by contract, permit, reviewer, or technical specification
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where its Chinese scope applies; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended unless made mandatory by procurement, project approval, or another instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are strong China-origin BESS evidence, but they are not Chile approvals. Use them in a documented crosswalk against the Chile project route.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform, China

rows.target_requirement: Chile BESS fire safety is usually handled through the project approval, electrical installation, owner engineering, insurer, municipal, environmental, and emergency-response route rather than a single BESS fire certificate. SEC installation rules, declared electrical installations, site permits, and project fire-risk studies may require evidence on thermal runaway, spacing, ventilation, detection, suppression, emergency shutdown, access, and Spanish emergency procedures.
target_standards: SEC Reglamento de Seguridad de las Instalaciones de Consumo de Energia Electrica - Decreto 08
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, container fire design, factory fire-suppression tests, and local Chinese project fire review. These documents may support Chilean due diligence but do not replace Chilean site permits, electrical declarations, insurer review, or emergency-response acceptance.
rows.gap: Exporters should localize the fire-safety package for Chile: installation drawings, rack spacing, ventilation or gas management, detection and suppression logic, deflagration or exhaust assumptions where relevant, shutdown interfaces, firefighter access, water runoff controls, SDS, incident response, and Spanish operating and emergency manuals.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Chilean electrical installation, permit, environmental, municipal, insurer, or emergency-response review applies; no universal Chile whole-BESS fire certificate is assumed
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval, installation location, and local fire review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Fire safety for Chile BESS is a site and project approval issue. Chinese GB reports help, but Chile-specific installation drawings, emergency procedures, and reviewer-ready fire-risk evidence are still needed.

source.publisher: Superintendencia de Electricidad y Combustibles (SEC), Chile

rows.requirement: CNE / Coordinador Storage and Grid Connection Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Chile's Law 21.505 amended the electricity law to promote electrical energy storage and allow pure storage systems to participate in the electricity market. Utility-scale BESS projects must address CNE technical rules and Coordinador Electrico Nacional connection, commissioning, operation, model, grid-code, and performance-verification processes, especially as large BESS deployment expands in solar-heavy northern areas including Atacama and Antofagasta.
target_standards: Norma Tecnica de Seguridad y Calidad de Servicio
Coordinador Electrico Nacional connection and verification procedures
rows.china_equivalent: China grid and GB/T storage documentation can help demonstrate technical capability, but it does not replace Chilean interconnection studies, CNE technical rules, Coordinador model validation, commissioning tests, telemetry, dispatch, and operational data requirements.
rows.gap: For Chile, prepare PSCAD/EMT and RMS models where requested, grid-forming or grid-following control descriptions, harmonic and fault-ride-through evidence, telemetry lists, commissioning test plans, availability guarantees, dispatch interfaces, and Chile-specific connection study responses.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects within CNE / Coordinador scope; project-specific for behind-the-meter or isolated systems depending on connection and permit route
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese grid connection, project approval, and dispatch requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chile BESS market access is strongly project and grid-route dependent. A China battery certificate does not close CNE / Coordinador connection, model, commissioning, or operational requirements.

source.publisher: Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile

rows.target_requirement: IEC 62619:2022 and the IEC 62933 series are internationally used references for industrial lithium battery safety and electrical energy storage systems. In Chile, they should be treated as standards that may be required by project specifications, lenders, EPC contracts, insurers, SEC technical review, or grid studies unless a specific Chilean instrument incorporates them for the product or installation.
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS packages often cite GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 for lithium batteries used in electrical energy storage. These are not one-for-one substitutes for IEC 62619:2022 or IEC 62933 evidence when a Chilean project contract or reviewer asks for IEC test clauses, system definitions, safety case documentation, or grid-storage performance evidence.
rows.gap: Map Chinese cell, module, rack, PCS, BMS, EMS, enclosure, HVAC, and fire-mitigation evidence to the exact IEC clauses requested by the Chile project. Where editions are not stated in the Chilean instrument or contract, cite bare standard numbers and confirm the edition with the buyer, certifier, or engineer of record.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless incorporated by a Chilean regulation, permit condition, grid requirement, contract, lender requirement, insurer requirement, or SEC decision
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where its Chinese scope applies; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended unless made mandatory by procurement, project approval, or another instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC evidence is commonly expected in bankable Chile BESS projects, but mandatory status depends on the exact Chilean instrument or contract. Confirm requested IEC clauses and editions before relying on China GB test reports.

rows.requirement: SEC Product Certification and Commercialization Authorization
rows.target_requirement: Chile's SEC manages mandatory certification lists, product protocols, foreign-certificate recognition, and commercialization authorization for electrical products. For BESS, exporters should not assume a universal whole-unit BESS certificate; instead, determine whether listed components such as inverters, protection equipment, meters, cables, or other electrical products require SEC certification or authorization before sale or installation.
target_standards: SEC Productos con Obligatoriedad de Certificacion
SEC Autorizacion de Comercializacion de Productos que Utilizan o Transportan Electricidad
rows.china_equivalent: China documentation commonly includes GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, inverter certificates, factory test reports, and battery module safety reports. These documents support technical review but do not by themselves prove Chile SEC product scope, Chilean certification, or authorization to commercialize regulated electrical products.
rows.gap: Prepare a Chile product-scope matrix by component, with HS codes, ratings, applicable SEC protocol checks, foreign certificates, test reports, Spanish labels and manuals, and a decision on certification, foreign-certificate recognition, or commercialization authorization where no authorized certification body route exists.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only for products and components within SEC mandatory certification or authorization scope; do not overclaim a universal whole-BESS mandatory certificate
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or voluntary depending on the Chinese product category, project use, and applicable GB / GB/T / certification route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Chile SEC compliance as a component-by-component scope exercise. China GB and factory documents are useful inputs but do not replace SEC certification, recognition, or commercialization authorization where a regulated electrical product is in scope.

source.publisher: Superintendencia de Electricidad y Combustibles (SEC), Chile

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Chile by sea, air, or land are normally handled under dangerous-goods transport rules that rely on the UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, subsection 38.3. BESS exporters should provide UN 38.3 test summaries, SDS, classification, packaging, state-of-charge controls where applicable, and transport documents for cells, modules, battery packs, and spare/replacement units.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, subsection 38.3
IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations lithium battery provisions
rows.china_equivalent: China factory GB reports, cell specifications, and battery safety certificates are not substitutes for dangerous-goods transport evidence. UN 38.3 reports and test summaries should match the actual cells, modules, and battery assemblies shipped from China to Chile.
rows.gap: Confirm the transported configuration, UN number, packing instruction, tested type, manufacturer identity, rated energy, and whether the project ships cells, modules, racks, containers, or replacement batteries. Re-test or obtain addenda if the shipped configuration is not covered by the existing UN 38.3 file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for dangerous-goods transport where lithium battery transport rules apply
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for export transport where lithium battery dangerous-goods rules apply

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 is a transport gate, not a Chile grid or SEC approval. It must match the shipped lithium battery configuration and should travel with the dangerous-goods documentation package.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
